events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

Categories will include: Digital Literacy 101, Online Safety (privacy, cyberbullying, online ethics, etc), Fake News, Balanced Screen Time (stats, mental health, etc.), & Cool Concepts (cool, futuristic things with digital lit e.g., AI).  There will be five questions ranging from 100 to 500 points for each category, as well as tiebreaker questions at the end.&nb

There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday SNAP Circuits
events.description: Come join us and explore electricity and circuitry. Learn about circuits and gain hands on experience with SNAP Circuits! Feel the spark when you build your own circuit! For children ages 6-12. Drop in.

events.title: Curious Chaos Club
events.description: Step into The Curious Chaos Club- where creativity gets a little messy (in the best way!) Kids ages 6- 12 can build, create and let the chaos begin!

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore a different STEM-themed activity every Sunday the library is open. Topics include robotics, circuitry, building and more! For children aged 6-12 and their caregivers. Drop in.

events.description: Visit the St. Lawrence branch for a Puppet Show & Stuffy Sleepover! Watch a puppet show and bring your stuffy to spend the night at the library. We are also celebrating the end of the TD Summer Reading Club.  For children ages 3 and up, their parents/caregivers and their stuffed animal friends. Only the stuffies will be sleeping over. events.title: Puppet Show: Goldilocks and the Three Bears
events.description: Join us for a puppet show adaptation of the classic English fairy tale, Goldilocks and the Three Bears (additional songs and stories too!). For children of all ages with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Therapy Animal Visit
events.description: Join us for small group visits with therapy animals. Children will have an opportunity to read, listen to stories and engage in therapeutic human-animal interaction. Experience the joy and calming benefits of a certified therapy animal from One Health Partners. Registration is required. Register online through the website, or call 416-393-7720 or visit the Riverdale Branch in person. Daycares and Camps, please call 416-393-7720 to inquire. events.title: Parent/Child Graphic Novel Book Club
events.description: Share the love of reading as members of our book club for kids and parents! Read a book together, then join us once a month at the library to chat about the book. Was it a great read? Or a total stinker? All opinions and thoughts are welcome and appreciated. For kids ages 9 to 12 and their grown-ups. No registration required. Copies will be available at the front desk on a first-come, first-served basis one month before each meeting.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

Space is limited.

events.title: Port Union Chess Club

Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Typing Practice for Kids & Teens
events.description: Typing is a key skill for writing reports, essays and other schoolwork. This program is intended to help children and teens who struggle with typing to practice using a keyboard and learn how to type faster and with increased accuracy. Participants will learn to type together using Tux typing which is afree and open source typing tutor created especially for children. Prerequisites: No computer experience required. events.title: Magic Mel’s Amazing Magic Show
events.description: Join us this month for a special Afterschool Club! Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as she leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fast-paced, comedy magic show for the entire family.

events.title: Income, Expense and Budget
events.description: Understanding how money flows in and out of your household is the foundation of good financial health. Presented by YMCA-GTA, this session, we’ll break down different types of income, common expenses, and how to create a realistic and effective budget. events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! Presented by Marlowe Andreyko from Dino Bites. This program is for ages 9+ .  Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Artists in the Library Youth Hub: Animate with 16mm film
events.description: Join us for one-of-a-kind workshop where you'll animate by hand on 16mm film to create your own short stop-motion piece. Participants will be taught an array of direct animation techniques to scratch, paint, draw, on a film strip. Community viewing on Saturday November 14th, 2-4 pm. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Dro

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. It is designed to help youth understand workplace expectations and succeed in their first roles. For teen sessions, bringing a resume is optional. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available.&nbs

events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Youth Hub: Paper Globe Lamps
events.description: Join us for a fun, illuminating program. Use watercolour paints, markers, tissue, and Mod Podge to create a unique ambient paper lampshade (lit with an electric tealight).  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! events.title: D&D for Teens: On-going Campaign
events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens . Space limited for players*, but watchers are welcome too. All supplies provided. Bring a 1st level character or pick a Pre-generated character. *Register in-person at the Youth Hub at 3:30PM. Fridays, 4:00-5:30PM October 2 October 23 November 27

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Programming in Robotics This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Seniors Celebrating Seniors: A Musical Walk Down Memory Lane
events.description: Join Jerry and Peter for an engaging and nostalgic musical experience featuring a rich selection of beloved songs from the 1950s and 1960s. Accomplished multi-instrumentalists, they will perform on a variety of instruments including guitar, bass, violin, and accordion, bringing timeless classics to life. This interactive, one-hour program invites participants to reminisce and share personal memories connected to the music, fostering a warm and inclusive atmosphere. events.title: Communication Badge Cross Stitch
events.description: Calling all Trekkies! Join us at the Merril Collection for an introductory cross stitch workshop.
